original_scenario:
Given: An applicant has assembled a complete Operational Authorisation pack with all required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, SORA evidence, insurance, crew credentials, etc.)
When: The applicant submits the pack to the CAA case officer intake system
Then: The system accepts the submission, opens a valid case, and transitions to Completeness Check (F02)

refined_specification.given: An applicant has assembled a complete Operational Authorisation pack with all required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, SORA evidence, insurance, crew credentials, etc.)
refined_specification.when: The applicant submits the Operational Authorisation pack the pack to the CAA case officer intake system
refined_specification.then: The system accepts the submission, opens a valid case, and transitions to Completeness Check (F02) per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: A unique case ID is generated, submitted timestamp is recorded, and case officer receives notification

original_scenario:
Given: A submitted pack undergoes automated validation against the completeness checklist
When: All required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, DAA evidence, insurance, crew training, etc.) are present
Then: System flags pack as COMPLETE and notifies case officer that case can proceed to SORA assessment

refined_specification.given: A submitted pack undergoes automated validation against the completeness checklist
refined_specification.when: All required artefacts (ConOps, ODD, DAA evidence, insurance, crew training, etc.) are present
refined_specification.then: System flags pack as COMPLETE and notifies case officer that case can proceed to SORA assessment per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case status becomes INTAKE_COMPLETE; timestamp recorded

original_scenario:
Given: A submitted pack is missing a critical artefact (e.g., SORA evidence or crew credentials)
When: Automated completeness check runs
Then: System flags the specific missing artefact, sets case to RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION status, and generates coaching feedback

refined_specification.given: A submitted pack is missing a critical artefact (e.g., SORA evidence or crew credentials)
refined_specification.when: Automated completeness validation runs runs
refined_specification.then: System flags the specific missing artefact, sets case to RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION status, and generates coaching feedback per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant receives structured feedback identifying exactly what is missing and deadline for resubmission

original_scenario:
Given: An applicant in Applicant Intake Mode has submitted partial artefacts (e.g., ConOps draft, incomplete SORA evidence)
When: Applicant requests coaching feedback before formal submission
Then: System provides targeted coaching on what is missing and quality issues without rejecting the case

refined_specification.given: An applicant in Applicant Intake Mode has submitted partial artefacts (e.g., ConOps draft, incomplete SORA evidence)
refined_specification.when: Applicant requests coaching feedback before formal submission
refined_specification.then: System provides targeted coaching on what is missing and quality issues without rejecting the case per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant retains ability to revise and resubmit before formal INTAKE_COMPLETE flag

original_scenario:
Given: A pack was returned for clarification due to missing artefacts; applicant has resubmitted revised pack
When: Second completeness check runs on resubmitted pack
Then: System validates all previously flagged items are now present and case transitions to INTAKE_COMPLETE

refined_specification.given: A pack was returned for clarification due to missing artefacts; applicant has resubmitted revised pack
refined_specification.when: Second completeness validation runs runs on resubmitted pack
refined_specification.then: System validates all previously flagged items are now present and case transitions to INTAKE_COMPLETE per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case officer is notified of successful re-submission; version history shows both original and revised submissions

original_scenario:
Given: Pack contains swarm-aware SORA evidence demonstrating coordinated multi-aircraft behaviour, inter-vehicle link robustness, and collective decision-making limits
When: SORA assessor reviews evidence and applies SORA 2.0 methodology with swarm context
Then: SAIL (Specific Assurance and Integrity Level) and OSO (Operational Safety Objectives) are derived defensibly

refined_specification.given: Pack contains swarm-aware SORA evidence demonstrating coordinated multi-aircraft behaviour, inter-vehicle link robustness, and collective decision-making limits
refined_specification.when: SORA assessor reviews evidence and applies SORA 2.0 methodology with swarm context
refined_specification.then: SAIL (Specific Assurance and Integrity Level) and OSO (Operational Safety Objectives) are derived defensibly per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Assessment record is created with clear traceability to swarm-specific evidence; SAIL/OSO documented in case file

original_scenario:
Given: Pack contains standard single-aircraft SORA evidence but lacks swarm-specific evidence on coordinated de-confliction and collective behaviour limits
When: SORA assessor attempts to derive SAIL using swarm context
Then: System flags insufficient swarm-aware evidence; case marked for expert panel review rather than auto-green

refined_specification.given: Pack contains standard single-aircraft SORA evidence but lacks swarm-specific evidence on coordinated de-confliction and collective behaviour limits
refined_specification.when: SORA assessor attempts to derive SAIL using swarm context
refined_specification.then: System flags insufficient swarm-aware evidence; case marked for expert panel review rather than auto-green per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: SORA assessor documents reason for manual escalation; applicant is notified of evidence gap

original_scenario:
Given: Initial SORA/SAIL derived; ConOps subsequently revised during phased trial (e.g., increased swarm size or autonomy scope)
When: Case gates to next phase with new operational envelope
Then: System triggers re-assessment of SORA with updated ConOps; previous SAIL may become invalid

refined_specification.given: Initial SORA/SAIL derived; ConOps subsequently revised during phased trial (e.g., increased swarm size or autonomy scope)
refined_specification.when: Case gates to next phase with new operational envelope
refined_specification.then: System triggers re-assessment of SORA with updated ConOps; previous SAIL may become invalid enabling defensible SAIL and OSO determination
refined_specification.and: Assessment history captures both original and revised SAIL determinations

original_scenario:
Given: Pack contains detailed ConOps (flight profiles, crew roles, decision procedures) and ODD (environmental limits, GNSS availability, airspace constraints)
When: Safety-case reviewer assesses ConOps and ODD clarity
Then: Reviewer confirms understanding of nominal swarm behaviour and identifies credible worst-case scenarios (link loss, geofence breach, non-cooperative encounter)

refined_specification.given: Pack contains detailed ConOps (flight profiles, crew roles, decision procedures) and ODD (environmental limits, GNSS availability, airspace constraints)
refined_specification.when: Safety-case reviewer assesses ConOps and ODD clarity
refined_specification.then: Reviewer confirms understanding of nominal swarm behaviour and identifies credible worst-case scenarios (link loss, geofence breach, non-cooperative encounter) per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: ConOps/ODD marked APPROVED; documented in case file with reviewer sign-off

original_scenario:
Given: ConOps submitted is vague on crew takeover procedures or contingency triggers
When: Safety-case reviewer reads ConOps
Then: Reviewer cannot confidently judge worst-credible behaviour; flags ConOps as INCOMPLETE

refined_specification.given: ConOps submitted is vague on crew takeover procedures or contingency triggers
refined_specification.when: Safety-case reviewer reads ConOps
refined_specification.then: Reviewer cannot confidently judge worst-credible behaviour; flags ConOps as INCOMPLETE per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant is requested to provide detailed failure mode procedures (e.g., link-loss response, geofence-trigger RTL) before proceeding

original_scenario:
Given: ConOps states operation in urban area with periodic GNSS loss; ODD does not explicitly define GNSS-denial procedures or swarm de-confliction alternatives
When: Safety reviewer assesses coverage of worst-credible GNSS-loss scenario
Then: Reviewer identifies gap: ODD does not bound behaviour during GNSS-denial; flag for clarification

refined_specification.given: ConOps states operation in urban area with periodic GNSS loss; ODD does not explicitly define GNSS-denial procedures or swarm de-confliction alternatives
refined_specification.when: Safety reviewer assesses coverage of worst-credible GNSS-loss scenario
refined_specification.then: Reviewer identifies gap: ODD does not bound behaviour during GNSS-denial; flag for clarification for crew training and safety case hazard analysis such that ConOps requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant must specify either: (a) geospatial exclusion of GNSS-poor zones, or (b) autonomous backup procedure (e.g., inertial-estimate + inter-vehicle ranging)

original_scenario:
Given: Applicant has submitted TDA request with airspace coordinates, time-window, and consultation package identifying stakeholders and prior-notification plan
When: Airspace regulator reviews TDA request and consultation evidence
Then: TDA is approved, NOTAM issued, and stakeholder consultation recorded; trial airspace is segregated from civilian traffic

refined_specification.given: Applicant has submitted TDA request with airspace coordinates, time-window, and consultation package identifying stakeholders and prior-notification plan
refined_specification.when: Airspace regulator reviews TDA request and consultation evidence
refined_specification.then: TDA is approved, NOTAM issued, and stakeholder consultation recorded; trial airspace is segregated from civilian traffic such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: TDA coordinates pinned to case file; NOTAM activation datetime set; consultation log archived

original_scenario:
Given: TDA request overlaps with existing procedure box or military airspace training corridor
When: Airspace regulator checks TDA against current airspace utilization
Then: Conflict detected; TDA cannot be approved as requested; regulator negotiates with conflicting user or proposes alternative time-window

refined_specification.given: TDA request overlaps with existing procedure box or military airspace training corridor
refined_specification.when: Airspace regulator checks TDA against current airspace utilization
refined_specification.then: Conflict detected; TDA cannot be approved as requested; regulator negotiates with conflicting user or proposes alternative time-window to segregate the trial from other airspace users such that ATSU requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case placed on hold; applicant is informed of conflict and offered remediation options

original_scenario:
Given: Consultation package does not identify nearby landowners, schools, or noise-sensitive sites per CAP 1616
When: Airspace regulator reviews stakeholder list
Then: Regulator flags consultation package as incomplete; case cannot proceed without comprehensive stakeholder identification

refined_specification.given: Consultation package does not identify nearby landowners, schools, or noise-sensitive sites per CAP 1616
refined_specification.when: Airspace regulator reviews stakeholder list
refined_specification.then: Regulator flags consultation package as incomplete; case cannot proceed without comprehensive stakeholder identification per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant is directed to conduct prior-notification planning per CAP 1616; case placed in RETURN_FOR_CLARIFICATION

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm aircraft drifts toward geofence boundary during autonomous flight; aircraft deviation from authorized area is within 50m tolerance
When: Onboard geofence enforcement system detects boundary approach
Then: Automatic Return-to-Launch (RTL) is triggered; all swarm members initiate coordinated return

refined_specification.given: Swarm aircraft drifts toward geofence boundary during autonomous flight; aircraft deviation from authorized area is within 50m tolerance
refined_specification.when: Onboard geofence enforcement system detects boundary approach
refined_specification.then: Automatic Return-to-Launch (RTL) is triggered; all swarm members initiate coordinated return to prevent fly-away and uncontrolled BVLOS such that geofence requirements are met
refined_specification.and: RTL execution is logged; case officer receives post-flight alert; incident marked as GEOFENCE_RTL_TRIGGERED

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm aircraft deviates from authorized area beyond 200m (configured tolerance); containment is at risk
When: Onboard geofence system detects breach beyond tolerance threshold
Then: Independent flight termination system is activated (independent of C2 link); all swarm members initiate rapid descent and safe power-down

refined_specification.given: Swarm aircraft deviates from authorized area beyond 200m (configured tolerance); containment is at risk
refined_specification.when: Onboard geofence system detects breach beyond tolerance threshold
refined_specification.then: Independent flight termination system is activated (independent of C2 link); all swarm members initiate rapid descent and safe power-down enabling defensible SAIL and OSO determination such that SORA requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Termination execution logged in forensic data; case officer alerted immediately; post-flight investigation triggered

original_scenario:
Given: Geofence enforcement system experiences hardware/software failure; boundary checking is unavailable
When: Swarm enters autonomous flight phase with failed geofence
Then: System detects geofence unavailability and initiates CONTINGENCY_MODE_ACTIVATION

refined_specification.given: Geofence enforcement system experiences hardware/software failure; boundary checking is unavailable
refined_specification.when: Swarm enters autonomous flight phase with failed geofence
refined_specification.then: System detects geofence unavailability and initiates CONTINGENCY_MODE_ACTIVATION to prevent fly-away and uncontrolled BVLOS such that containment requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Crew is immediately notified of geofence loss; flight is aborted and aircraft diverted to safe landing zone per ConOps contingency

original_scenario:
Given: Geofence configured with three zones: (1) primary trial boundary, (2) intermediate caution zone 100m inside, (3) inner safe-zone with crew stations
When: Aircraft trajectory approaches each zone boundary
Then: Zone-specific actions triggered: caution zone → reduced autonomy + operator alert; primary boundary → RTL; breach → termination

refined_specification.given: Geofence configured with three zones: (1) primary trial boundary, (2) intermediate caution zone 100m inside, (3) inner safe-zone with crew stations
refined_specification.when: Aircraft trajectory approaches each zone boundary
refined_specification.then: Zone-specific actions triggered: caution zone → reduced autonomy + operator alert; primary boundary → RTL; breach → termination to prevent fly-away and uncontrolled BVLOS such that geofence requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Scenario data:

original_scenario:
Given: C2 link experiences momentary outage (100ms–1s) during autonomous swarm flight due to electromagnetic interference or multipath fading
When: Link loss detected by onboard monitor; swarm continues autonomous operation
Then: Swarm members rely on inter-vehicle links and pre-planned autonomy; link recovery occurs within hold-safe timeout

refined_specification.given: C2 link experiences momentary outage (100ms–1s) during autonomous swarm flight due to electromagnetic interference or multipath fading
refined_specification.when: Link loss detected by onboard monitor; swarm continues autonomous operation
refined_specification.then: Swarm members rely on inter-vehicle links and pre-planned autonomy; link recovery occurs within hold-safe timeout to ensure link loss is survivable and non-interfering such that C2 link requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Link loss event logged; case officer receives post-flight notification of outage duration and recovery; no safety impact

original_scenario:
Given: C2 link remains unavailable for > 10 seconds (configured threshold) during flight
When: Loss duration exceeds hold-safe timeout
Then: Swarm enters CONTINGENCY_MODE: autonomous navigation via pre-planned waypoints or inter-vehicle consensus continues until RTL is triggered

refined_specification.given: C2 link remains unavailable for > 10 seconds (configured threshold) during flight
refined_specification.when: Loss duration exceeds hold-safe timeout
refined_specification.then: Swarm enters CONTINGENCY_MODE: autonomous navigation via pre-planned waypoints or inter-vehicle consensus continues until RTL is triggered to ensure link loss is survivable and non-interfering such that C2 link requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Flight is diverted to predetermined safe landing zone; crew is unable to command but swarm remains safe and recoverable

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm is executing distributed consensus maneuver (e.g., coordinated altitude change); C2 link is lost
When: Loss occurs mid-maneuver when inter-vehicle coordination is critical
Then: Swarm members lose ground control authority but maintain inter-vehicle links; they follow distributed contingency algorithm to safely freeze maneuver

refined_specification.given: Swarm is executing distributed consensus maneuver (e.g., coordinated altitude change); C2 link is lost
refined_specification.when: Loss occurs mid-maneuver when inter-vehicle coordination is critical
refined_specification.then: Swarm members lose ground control authority but maintain inter-vehicle links; they follow distributed contingency algorithm to safely freeze maneuver to ensure link loss is survivable and non-interfering such that C2 link requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Post-flight analysis shows whether consensus algorithm successfully prevented collision; high-risk scenario documented in lessons-learned

original_scenario:
Given: C2 system transmits on authorized frequency with approved power level; spectrum monitoring equipment is in place
When: Spectrum specialist reviews monitoring data and adjacent-frequency user complaints
Then: No harmful interference detected; UAS emissions are within approved limits; spectrum coexistence confirmed

refined_specification.given: C2 system transmits on authorized frequency with approved power level; spectrum monitoring equipment is in place
refined_specification.when: Spectrum specialist reviews monitoring data and adjacent-frequency user complaints
refined_specification.then: No harmful interference detected; UAS emissions are within approved limits; spectrum coexistence confirmed to ensure link loss is survivable and non-interfering such that C2 link requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Monitoring report generated; compliance verified in case file

original_scenario:
Given: One aircraft loses RF link to its neighboring swarm members but maintains C2 link to ground
When: Inter-vehicle link timeout occurs
Then: Affected aircraft switches to ground-only contingency mode; ground crew re-establishes command authority

refined_specification.given: One aircraft loses RF link to its neighboring swarm members but maintains C2 link to ground
refined_specification.when: Inter-vehicle link timeout occurs
refined_specification.then: Affected aircraft switches to ground-only contingency mode; ground crew re-establishes command authority to ensure link loss is survivable and non-interfering such that C2 link requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Remaining swarm members continue coordinated operation; separated aircraft is safely recovered or redirected

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm encounters another aircraft equipped with transponder (cooperative); position, altitude, and velocity known via ADS-B
When: DAA system detects cooperative intruder and initiates avoidance
Then: Avoidance maneuver (altitude change or lateral displacement) is executed autonomously; safe separation is maintained per DO-365 standards

refined_specification.given: Swarm encounters another aircraft equipped with transponder (cooperative); position, altitude, and velocity known via ADS-B
refined_specification.when: DAA system detects cooperative intruder and initiates avoidance
refined_specification.then: Avoidance maneuver (altitude change or lateral displacement) is executed autonomously; safe separation is maintained per DO-365 standards such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Encounter logged with intruder track data; post-flight analysis confirms resolution was predictable and safe

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm encounters an aircraft without transponder (non-cooperative, position unknown); detected only by onboard radar/vision
When: DAA system senses intruder and initiates evasive maneuver
Then: Swarm executes conservative avoidance (e.g., altitude climb); encounter is resolved without collision

refined_specification.given: Swarm encounters an aircraft without transponder (non-cooperative, position unknown); detected only by onboard radar/vision
refined_specification.when: DAA system senses intruder and initiates evasive maneuver
refined_specification.then: Swarm executes conservative avoidance (e.g., altitude climb); encounter is resolved without collision such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Post-flight analysis shows conservatism was sufficient; encounter logged as resolved safely despite information asymmetry

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm is executing formation maneuver; external intruder is detected; swarm must simultaneously avoid intruder and maintain internal separation
When: DAA triggers avoidance; swarm consensus algorithm must coordinate avoidance without internal collision
Then: Coordinated de-confliction ensures swarm members all execute compatible avoidance; safe separation maintained both internally and from intruder

refined_specification.given: Swarm is executing formation maneuver; external intruder is detected; swarm must simultaneously avoid intruder and maintain internal separation
refined_specification.when: DAA triggers avoidance; swarm consensus algorithm must coordinate avoidance without internal collision
refined_specification.then: Coordinated de-confliction ensures swarm members all execute compatible avoidance; safe separation maintained both internally and from intruder such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Encounter logged with inter-aircraft separation metrics; demonstrates coordinated swarm DAA capability

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm is operating in GNSS-denied area (urban canyon, indoor); DAA is primary separation mechanism
When: Encounter is detected via onboard radar/vision in GNSS-denied corner case
Then: DAA relies on local sensor data only (no GPS-aided fusion); avoidance is executed conservatively

refined_specification.given: Swarm is operating in GNSS-denied area (urban canyon, indoor); DAA is primary separation mechanism
refined_specification.when: Encounter is detected via onboard radar/vision in GNSS-denied corner case
refined_specification.then: DAA relies on local sensor data only (no GPS-aided fusion); avoidance is executed conservatively such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Post-flight analysis shows whether GNSS-denial impacts avoidance confidence; logs captured for forensic review

original_scenario:
Given: Onboard DAA system experiences hardware/sensor failure; encounter detection is unavailable
When: Intruder approaches swarm (cooperative or non-cooperative)
Then: System detects DAA unavailability; flight is immediately aborted and swarm is diverted to nearest safe landing

refined_specification.given: Onboard DAA system experiences hardware/sensor failure; encounter detection is unavailable
refined_specification.when: Intruder approaches swarm (cooperative or non-cooperative)
refined_specification.then: System detects DAA unavailability; flight is immediately aborted and swarm is diverted to nearest safe landing such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Crew is alerted; incident is investigated; case may require DAA system redesign or additional operational constraints

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm architecture is distributed consensus-based (no single lead); airborne limits enforce velocity, altitude, and heading constraints; emergent behaviour test data demonstrates predictability
When: Autonomy reviewer assesses architecture documentation and test evidence
Then: Reviewer confirms collective behaviour meets ODD bounds; emergent phenomena (e.g., adaptive formation tightening) are explained and modeled

refined_specification.given: Swarm architecture is distributed consensus-based (no single lead); airborne limits enforce velocity, altitude, and heading constraints; emergent behaviour test data demonstrates predictability
refined_specification.when: Autonomy reviewer assesses architecture documentation and test evidence
refined_specification.then: Reviewer confirms collective behaviour meets ODD bounds; emergent phenomena (e.g., adaptive formation tightening) are explained and modeled for crew training and safety case hazard analysis such that ConOps requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Architecture marked APPROVED; emergent behaviour evidence linked to case file

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm architecture enforces local velocity/altitude limits; test evidence shows undesired emergent behaviour (e.g., collective oscillation, deadlock in consensus)
When: Autonomy reviewer analyzes emergent behaviour test results
Then: Undesired behaviour identified; reviewer flags architecture as INCOMPLETE

refined_specification.given: Swarm architecture enforces local velocity/altitude limits; test evidence shows undesired emergent behaviour (e.g., collective oscillation, deadlock in consensus)
refined_specification.when: Autonomy reviewer analyzes emergent behaviour test results
refined_specification.then: Undesired behaviour identified; reviewer flags architecture as INCOMPLETE per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant must either: (a) redesign consensus algorithm, (b) tighten airborne limits further, or (c) add ground-based override mechanism

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm architecture uses designate lead aircraft for decision-making; failover protocol transfers authority to ground crew if lead is incapacitated
When: Autonomy reviewer assesses lead-aircraft architecture and failover procedure
Then: Reviewer validates that leadership failover maintains swarm cohesion and prevents collision during transition

refined_specification.given: Swarm architecture uses designate lead aircraft for decision-making; failover protocol transfers authority to ground crew if lead is incapacitated
refined_specification.when: Autonomy reviewer assesses lead-aircraft architecture and failover procedure
refined_specification.then: Reviewer validates that leadership failover maintains swarm cohesion and prevents collision during transition to assure collective behavior and emergent failure modes such that emergent behavior requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Failover procedure documented; test results confirm safe transition; architecture approved

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
original_scenario:
Given: Pack includes AMLAS/SACE assurance case for ML components (e.g., environmental classification, traffic prediction); frozen-model manifest documents exact model version, training data, and performance metrics; explainability framework (SHAP, attention maps) is documented
When: ML assurance specialist reviews AMLAS case and manifest
Then: Reviewer verifies model version is pinned and reproducible; explainability evidence supports forensic audit trail

refined_specification.given: Pack includes AMLAS/SACE assurance case for ML components (e.g., environmental classification, traffic prediction); frozen-model manifest documents exact model version, training data, and performance metrics; explainability framework (SHAP, attention maps) is documented
refined_specification.when: ML assurance specialist reviews AMLAS case and manifest
refined_specification.then: Reviewer verifies model version is pinned and reproducible; explainability evidence supports forensic audit trail such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: ML assurance marked APPROVED; manifest stored in immutable archive; explainability evidence linked to case file

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
original_scenario:
Given: Swarm uses ML model for real-time decision-making (e.g., autonomy mode selection); incident occurs; post-incident investigation requires forensic explanation of ML decision
When: Investigator attempts to trace why model made specific decision
Then: Explainability evidence is absent or sparse; investigator cannot confidently explain decision to regulator

refined_specification.given: Swarm uses ML model for real-time decision-making (e.g., autonomy mode selection); incident occurs; post-incident investigation requires forensic explanation of ML decision
refined_specification.when: Investigator attempts to trace why model made specific decision
refined_specification.then: Explainability evidence is absent or sparse; investigator cannot confidently explain decision to regulator to assure collective behavior and emergent failure modes such that swarm requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case marked CRITICAL FINDING: ML forensic explainability is insufficient; design must include per-decision explanation logging

feature: F10: ML/AI Assurance Case & Model Manifest
original_scenario:
Given: Case closes; post-trial audit discovers deployed model version differs from manifest-documented version
When: Audit compares manifest record to actual deployed code
Then: Version mismatch flagged; case integrity questioned; forensic confidence reduced

refined_specification.given: Case closes; post-trial audit discovers deployed model version differs from manifest-documented version
refined_specification.when: Audit compares manifest record to actual deployed code
refined_specification.then: Version mismatch flagged; case integrity questioned; forensic confidence reduced such that ML requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Finding logged; lessons-learned note added regarding manifest-synchronization procedures; process improvement recommended

original_scenario:
Given: Human factors assessment shows crew mental workload (NASA-TLX or equivalent) remains below 70% during nominal and off-nominal scenarios; take-over procedure requires < 3 seconds to resume command
When: Human factors inspector reviews workload assessment and take-over procedure documentation
Then: Inspector confirms meaningful human control is demonstrated; crew can perceive, understand, and intervene in safety-critical scenarios

refined_specification.given: Human factors assessment shows crew mental workload (NASA-TLX or equivalent) remains below 70% during nominal and off-nominal scenarios; take-over procedure requires < 3 seconds to resume command
refined_specification.when: Human factors inspector reviews workload assessment and take-over procedure documentation
refined_specification.then: Inspector confirms meaningful human control is demonstrated; crew can perceive, understand, and intervene in safety-critical scenarios such that ConOps requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Human oversight marked APPROVED; workload metrics documented in case file

original_scenario:
Given: Simulation/test data shows crew workload exceeds 85% during simultaneous encounter + contingency scenario
When: Human factors inspector analyzes workload data
Then: Inspector flags workload as EXCESSIVE; meaningful human control is questioned for this scenario

refined_specification.given: Simulation/test data shows crew workload exceeds 85% during simultaneous encounter + contingency scenario
refined_specification.when: Human factors inspector analyzes workload data
refined_specification.then: Inspector flags workload as EXCESSIVE; meaningful human control is questioned for this scenario such that DAA requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant must either: (a) simplify crew procedures, (b) add automation to reduce workload, or (c) restrict operation to exclude this scenario from trial envelope

original_scenario:
Given: ConOps references crew take-over capability but does not specify procedure; no test evidence of actual take-over feasibility
When: Human factors inspector requests take-over procedure and test results
Then: Applicant cannot provide tested procedure; take-over time and success rate remain unverified

refined_specification.given: ConOps references crew take-over capability but does not specify procedure; no test evidence of actual take-over feasibility
refined_specification.when: Human factors inspector requests take-over procedure and test results
refined_specification.then: Applicant cannot provide tested procedure; take-over time and success rate remain unverified for crew training and safety case hazard analysis such that ConOps requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case marked INCOMPLETE; meaningful human control cannot be demonstrated; procedural redesign required

original_scenario:
Given: Swarm experiences dual failure: C2 link loss + autonomy fault; independent termination system activates per geofence trigger
When: On-board termination trigger fires (geofence breach or energy threshold)
Then: All aircraft simultaneously power down and initiate safe descent; recovery is complete within impact zone

refined_specification.given: Swarm experiences dual failure: C2 link loss + autonomy fault; independent termination system activates per geofence trigger
refined_specification.when: On-board termination trigger fires (geofence breach or energy threshold)
refined_specification.then: All aircraft simultaneously power down and initiate safe descent; recovery is complete within impact zone per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Termination event logged; case officer alerted; incident classified as CONTROLLED_TERMINATION

original_scenario:
Given: C2 link lost during flight; contingency matrix specifies response: 'link loss > 10s → navigate to nearest safe landing waypoint'
When: Swarm recognizes link loss and timeout triggers contingency navigation
Then: Pre-planned waypoint sequence executes; swarm navigates autonomously to safe landing site per ConOps

refined_specification.given: C2 link lost during flight; contingency matrix specifies response: 'link loss > 10s → navigate to nearest safe landing waypoint'
refined_specification.when: Swarm recognizes link loss and timeout triggers contingency navigation
refined_specification.then: Pre-planned waypoint sequence executes; swarm navigates autonomously to safe landing site per ConOps for crew training and safety case hazard analysis such that ODD requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Contingency execution logged; post-flight analysis confirms waypoint selection was safe; incident classified as CONTINGENCY_SUCCESS

original_scenario:
Given: Incident occurs that is not enumerated in contingency matrix (e.g., unexpected swarm member incapacitation while others are airborne)
When: Crew attempts to follow contingency procedures but finds no matching scenario
Then: Crew must improvise; flight outcome is uncertain; post-incident review reveals gap in contingency planning

refined_specification.given: Incident occurs that is not enumerated in contingency matrix (e.g., unexpected swarm member incapacitation while others are airborne)
refined_specification.when: Crew attempts to follow contingency procedures but finds no matching scenario
refined_specification.then: Crew must improvise; flight outcome is uncertain; post-incident review reveals gap in contingency planning to assure collective behavior and emergent failure modes such that swarm requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Case finding: contingency matrix is incomplete; design must include more comprehensive off-nominal scenario enumeration

original_scenario:
Given: Trial operator has coordinated with ATSU; TDA is activated, NOTAM issued, and ATC is aware of trial
When: Flight commences within TDA boundaries
Then: ATSU maintains awareness; any conflicting traffic is diverted outside TDA; trial proceeds without air traffic conflict

refined_specification.given: Trial operator has coordinated with ATSU; TDA is activated, NOTAM issued, and ATC is aware of trial
refined_specification.when: Flight commences within TDA boundaries
refined_specification.then: ATSU maintains awareness; any conflicting traffic is diverted outside TDA; trial proceeds without air traffic conflict to segregate the trial from other airspace users
refined_specification.and: ATSU coordination log recorded; case file documents ATC liaison

original_scenario:
Given: During flight, one swarm member reports rapid altitude loss; structural damage or battery failure suspected
When: Swarm de-confliction algorithm detects the failing member
Then: Remaining swarm members immediately increase separation; failing member's trajectory is calculated; emergency termination considered if landing is not viable

refined_specification.given: During flight, one swarm member reports rapid altitude loss; structural damage or battery failure suspected
refined_specification.when: Swarm de-confliction algorithm detects the failing member
refined_specification.then: Remaining swarm members immediately increase separation; failing member's trajectory is calculated; emergency termination considered if landing is not viable such that cooperative and non-cooperative encounters are resolved predictably
refined_specification.and: Incident logged; off-nominal response tested post-flight; contingency procedure revised if inadequate

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
original_scenario:
Given: During trial, a minor incident occurs (e.g., unexplained brief altitude drop); complete flight data is recorded continuously at 100Hz for aircraft state, crew commands, autonomy decisions, and external events
When: Post-flight analysis begins; investigator reviews recorded data
Then: Complete dataset enables root cause analysis; incident is classified for MOR threshold assessment

refined_specification.given: During trial, a minor incident occurs (e.g., unexplained brief altitude drop); complete flight data is recorded continuously at 100Hz for aircraft state, crew commands, autonomy decisions, and external events
refined_specification.when: Post-flight analysis begins; investigator reviews recorded data
refined_specification.then: Complete dataset enables root cause analysis; incident is classified for MOR threshold assessment per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: If MOR-reportable, formal report is filed with CAA within required timeframe; data archive is retained per regulations

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
original_scenario:
Given: Incident occurs (near-collision); post-flight review discovers recording was interrupted due to disk full or sensor failure
When: Investigator attempts to reconstruct incident sequence
Then: Critical time-window of data is absent; investigator cannot confidently determine root cause

refined_specification.given: Incident occurs (near-collision); post-flight review discovers recording was interrupted due to disk full or sensor failure
refined_specification.when: Investigator attempts to reconstruct incident sequence
refined_specification.then: Critical time-window of data is absent; investigator cannot confidently determine root cause to make the trial safe in any off-nominal state such that independent termination requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Finding: data recording completeness is insufficient; design must ensure continuous recording with redundancy

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
original_scenario:

feature: F13: Incident Data Recording & MOR Handling
original_scenario:
Given: Incident occurs that is borderline MOR-reportable (e.g., mild structural damage, no injury); guidance is unclear on threshold
When: Applicant/operator must decide whether to file MOR
Then: Conservative approach taken: MOR is filed to be safe; CAA reviews and either classifies as MOR or closes as non-reportable

refined_specification.then: Conservative approach taken: MOR is filed to be safe; CAA reviews and either classifies as MOR or closes as non-reportable to enable forensic investigation and GDPR compliance such that recorded evidence requirements are met

original_scenario:

original_scenario:
Given: Insurance policy submitted is for general aviation; exclusion clause states 'unmanned aircraft systems excluded from coverage'
When: Authorisation officer reviews policy details
Then: Insurance is flagged as INADEQUATE; policy does not cover trial operations

original_scenario:
Given: Ground survey documents trial area: rural location, nearest residence 2km away, no schools or hospitals within 5km, no critical infrastructure overhead

Then: Risk assessment is factually grounded in observed conditions; third-party exposure is low; survey is marked APPROVED

original_scenario:
Given: Ground survey is submitted but does not identify power transmission lines, cellular towers, or critical facilities near trial area

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
original_scenario:

Then: All competency domains are verified; crew is qualified for trial scope; credentials marked APPROVED

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
original_scenario:
Given: Pilot holds commercial UAS license but training records show no swarm-specific instruction or type rating
When: Licensing inspector assesses crew competency
Then: Inspector flags missing swarm operations qualification; crew is not approved for swarm trial

refined_specification.when: Licensing inspector assesses crew competency
refined_specification.then: Inspector flags missing swarm operations qualification; crew is not approved for swarm trial to assure collective behavior and emergent failure modes such that emergent behavior requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant must enroll pilot in swarm operations training course; case cannot proceed until competency is demonstrated

feature: F16: Crew Training & Competency Evidence Review
original_scenario:
Given: Crew training completed 5 years ago; no recent recurrency training or checkride on file
When: Licensing inspector reviews training recency
Then: Inspector flags training as STALE; competency currency cannot be assumed

refined_specification.given: Crew training completed 5 years ago; no recent recurrency training or checkride on file
refined_specification.when: Licensing inspector reviews training recency
refined_specification.then: Inspector flags training as STALE; competency currency cannot be assumed per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Applicant must provide evidence of recent recurrency training (within past 12 months); case cannot open without current credentials

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature

feature: F17: CAA/MAA Apportionment & Duty Holder Signature

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
original_scenario:
Given: Phase 1 is defined as: max 2 aircraft, pre-planned waypoints, segregated airspace, no autonomous decision-making; evidence threshold for Phase 1 is: 10 successful flights, zero incidents
When: Applicant requests Phase 2 progression after completing Phase 1 evidence requirements
Then: Independent panel reviews Phase 1 evidence (10 flight logs, post-flight summaries, sensor data); all criteria met

refined_specification.given: Phase 1 is defined as: max 2 aircraft, pre-planned waypoints, segregated airspace, no autonomous decision-making; evidence threshold for Phase 1 is: 10 successful flights, zero incidents
refined_specification.when: Applicant requests Phase 2 progression after completing Phase 1 evidence requirements
refined_specification.then: Independent panel reviews Phase 1 evidence (10 flight logs, post-flight summaries, sensor data); all criteria met to segregate the trial from other airspace users such that TDA requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Panel approves Phase 2 progression; Phase 2 (max 4 aircraft, increased autonomy) is authorized; evidence begins for Phase 2

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
original_scenario:
Given: During Phase 2 flights, a near-miss incident occurs involving geofence malfunction; evidence suggests Phase 2 autonomy level is not adequately validated
When: Panel reviews incident and Phase 2 evidence completeness
Then: Panel fails Phase 2 gate due to incident; Phase 2 authorization is withdrawn

refined_specification.given: During Phase 2 flights, a near-miss incident occurs involving geofence malfunction; evidence suggests Phase 2 autonomy level is not adequately validated
refined_specification.when: Panel reviews incident and Phase 2 evidence completeness
refined_specification.then: Panel fails Phase 2 gate due to incident; Phase 2 authorization is withdrawn per regulatory standards, recording case ID and submission timestamp such that Operational Authorisation pack requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Trial reverts to Phase 1 operations; applicant must investigate incident, redesign containment logic, and accumulate additional evidence before Phase 2 re-attempt

feature: F18: Phased Trial Gates & Progressive Evidence Build
original_scenario:
Given: Phase 2 gate initially failed due to geofence issue; applicant has redesigned geofence logic and conducted 10 new validation flights
When: Applicant resubmits Phase 2 evidence after remediation
Then: Panel reviews new evidence (10 additional flights, geofence redesign analysis); all pass criteria are met

refined_specification.given: Phase 2 gate initially failed due to geofence issue; applicant has redesigned geofence logic and conducted 10 new validation flights
refined_specification.when: Applicant resubmits Phase 2 evidence after remediation
refined_specification.then: Panel reviews new evidence (10 additional flights, geofence redesign analysis); all pass criteria are met to prevent fly-away and uncontrolled BVLOS such that containment requirements are met
refined_specification.and: Phase 2 gate is re-passed; Phase 2 authorization is restored; trial progression continues
